title: SAP Authorization Flow (Classic ABAP)
description: How a single AUTHORITY-CHECK statement resolves against a user's session authorization buffer, built at logon from role/profile assignments.
mermaid:
flowchart TD
  A[User Logon] --> B[Kernel reads AGR_USERS / UST04]
  B --> C[Build Authorization Buffer in Session Memory]
  C --> D[User Executes Transaction]
  D --> E[AUTHORITY-CHECK Statement in Code]
  E --> F{Value in Buffer?}
  F -->|Yes| G[sy-subrc = 0, Continue]
  F -->|No| H[sy-subrc <> 0, Error/SU53 Entry]
  I[Role Change] -.requires re-login/SU56.-> C

title: Fiori Authorization Flow
description: The two independent halves of Fiori access: front-end catalog/tile visibility and back-end OData authorization, both required for a working app.
mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant U as User
  participant FLP as Fiori Launchpad
  participant GW as Gateway/OData
  participant BE as Backend ABAP
  U->>FLP: Logon
  FLP->>FLP: Check Catalog/Space Assignment
  FLP-->>U: Render Tile (if assigned)
  U->>GW: Open App - GET Entity Set
  GW->>GW: Check SICF Service Active
  GW->>BE: Forward Request
  BE->>BE: AUTHORITY-CHECK on OData Object
  BE-->>U: Data or 403

title: SAP Gateway Flow (OData / Batch)
description: How Gateway's IWFND and IWBEP layers process an OData call, including batch sub-operation handling.
mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A[HTTP/OData Request] --> B[IWFND - Front-End]
  B --> C{SICF Node Active?}
  C -->|No| D[404 Not Found]
  C -->|Yes| E[IWBEP - Back-End Enablement]
  E --> F[Backend ABAP / CDS Logic]
  F --> G[AUTHORITY-CHECK]
  G -->|Batch Request| H[Each Sub-Operation Checked Independently]
  G -->|Single Call| I[Direct Response]

title: SAP BTP Authentication & Authorization Flow
description: How a BTP application authenticates a user via IAS and authorizes API calls via XSUAA-issued scoped tokens.
mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant U as User
  participant App as BTP Application
  participant IAS as SAP IAS
  participant XS as XSUAA
  U->>App: Access Request
  App->>IAS: Redirect for Authentication
  IAS-->>App: SAML/OIDC Assertion
  App->>XS: Exchange for Token
  XS-->>App: JWT with Scopes
  App->>App: Validate Scope per API Call
  App-->>U: Response

title: OAuth 2.0 SAML Bearer Assertion Flow
description: How a cloud application obtains a backend-scoped token on behalf of a specific user via SAML Bearer Assertion, enabling Principal Propagation.
mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant App as Cloud App
  participant IAS as IAS (Assertion Issuer)
  participant BE as On-Premise OAuth Server
  App->>IAS: Request SAML Assertion for User
  IAS-->>App: Signed SAML Assertion
  App->>BE: Present Assertion (SAML Bearer Grant)
  BE->>BE: Validate Trust + Resolve Local User Mapping
  BE-->>App: Scoped Access Token for Propagated User
  App->>BE: API Call with Access Token

title: SAML2 SP/IdP Assertion Exchange
description: The classic SAML2 web SSO flow between an ABAP system acting as Service Provider and an Identity Provider.
mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant U as User Browser
  participant SP as SAP System (Service Provider)
  participant IdP as Identity Provider
  U->>SP: Access Protected Resource
  SP-->>U: Redirect to IdP (AuthnRequest)
  U->>IdP: Authenticate
  IdP-->>U: Signed SAML Assertion
  U->>SP: POST Assertion to ACS URL
  SP->>SP: Validate Signature via STRUST + Map User
  SP-->>U: Session Granted

title: Principal Propagation Flow
description: The full chain preserving a cloud user's identity through Cloud Connector into an on-premise backend call.
mermaid:
flowchart TD
  A[Cloud App - User Authenticated via IAS] --> B[XSUAA Issues Token]
  B --> C[Cloud Connector]
  C --> D{System + Resource Allowlisted?}
  D -->|No| E[Connection Rejected]
  D -->|Yes| F[Forward to On-Premise Backend]
  F --> G{Principal Propagation Trust Valid?}
  G -->|No| H[Identity Resolution Error]
  G -->|Yes| I[Backend Resolves Local User Mapping]
  I --> J[AUTHORITY-CHECK as Propagated User]

title: Cloud Connector Access Control Flow
description: How Cloud Connector enforces system-level and resource-level access control lists on outbound-initiated tunnels.
mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A[On-Premise Network] --> B[Cloud Connector Agent]
  B -->|Outbound-Initiated Tunnel| C[BTP Subaccount]
  D[Cloud App API Call] --> C
  C --> B
  B --> E{System in ACL?}
  E -->|No| F[Rejected]
  E -->|Yes| G{Resource Path in ACL?}
  G -->|No| F
  G -->|Yes| H[Forward to Backend System]

title: IAS Federation to Corporate Identity Provider
description: How IAS acts as both a Service Provider (to the corporate IdP) and an Identity Provider (to downstream applications) simultaneously.
mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant U as User
  participant App as Relying Application
  participant IAS as SAP IAS
  participant Corp as Corporate IdP
  U->>App: Access Request
  App->>IAS: Redirect (App trusts IAS)
  IAS->>Corp: Redirect (IAS trusts Corporate IdP)
  Corp-->>IAS: Assertion
  IAS->>IAS: Evaluate Conditional Authentication
  IAS-->>App: New Assertion/Token (IAS as IdP)
  App-->>U: Access Granted

title: IPS Provisioning Flow
description: How Identity Provisioning Service reads from a source system, applies filters and transformations, and writes to one or more targets via SCIM or RFC.
mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A[Source System - HR/AD/IAS] -->|Read| B[IPS Job]
  B --> C{Filter Rule}
  C -->|Excluded| D[No Action - Silent]
  C -->|Included| E[Apply Attribute Mapping/Transform]
  E -->|SCIM| F[Target: IAS / BTP Subaccount]
  E -->|RFC/BAPI| G[Target: S/4HANA]
  B --> H[Job Log: Per-Record Success/Failure]

title: GRC Access Request Management Flow
description: The preventive access-request pathway in SAP GRC, running risk analysis before provisioning rather than only detecting violations afterward.
mermaid:
flowchart TD
  A[New Access Request] --> B[GRC Access Request Management]
  B --> C[Risk Analysis Against Rule Set]
  C --> D{Conflict Found?}
  D -->|No| E[Auto/Approved Provisioning]
  D -->|Yes| F[Approval Workflow]
  F --> G{Mitigate or Remediate?}
  G -->|Mitigate| H[Assign Mitigating Control + Recertify]
  G -->|Remediate| I[Adjust Request - Remove Conflicting Access]
  H --> E
  I --> E

title: PFCG Role Creation Flow
description: The end-to-end lifecycle of building a role: menu assembly, SU24-driven proposals, value maintenance, generation, and assignment.
mermaid:
flowchart TD
  A[Add Transactions to Role Menu] --> B[PFCG Reads SU24 Check Indicators]
  B --> C[Proposed Authorization Objects Populated]
  C --> D[Security Consultant Maintains/Narrows Values]
  D --> E[Organizational Level Values Entered]
  E --> F[Generate Profile]
  F --> G[Assign Role to User with Validity Dates]
  G --> H[User Buffer Updated at Next Logon]
